Descriptor unwrapping: isinstance(fn, staticmethod) dispatches on Python's descriptor protocol to extract __func__ before wrapping. Not a trust boundary — structural dispatch on known descriptor types.

Descriptor unwrapping: isinstance(fn, classmethod) dispatches on Python's descriptor protocol to extract __func__ before wrapping. Not a trust boundary — structural dispatch on known descriptor types.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(tier, int) validates the _wardline_tier attribute type during TierStamped construction. This IS the enforcement — type-checking is the security primitive.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(tier, int) validates tier parameter at the stamp_tier API boundary. Guards against callers passing non-int tiers.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(obj, TierStamped) checks whether to unwrap a tier-stamped wrapper. Structural dispatch on the wardline protocol type.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(tier, int) validates the type of a runtime _wardline_tier attribute before numeric comparison. Defensive guard in the enforcement hot path.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(obj, ValidatedRecord) checks protocol conformance. This is the ValidatedRecord verification entry point — isinstance is the correct primitive.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(tier, int) validates _wardline_tier type after ValidatedRecord protocol check. Defense-in-depth within the enforcement function.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(tier, int) guards the ternary in _invoke_on_violation call. Prevents passing non-int to tier parameter.
Enforcement mechanism: isinstance(groups, (tuple, set, frozenset)) validates _wardline_groups type. Final validation step in the ValidatedRecord check.
Manifest coherence: isinstance(c, dict) guards against malformed contract entries in parsed YAML. Defensive check at the manifest-to-internal boundary.
Manifest coherence: isinstance(dec_raw, str) validates parsed decorator restoration evidence is a string before comparison. Guards against non-string values from annotation extraction.
Manifest coherence: isinstance(overlay_raw, str) validates parsed overlay restoration evidence is a string before comparison. Guards against non-string values from manifest parsing.
Manifest merge: isinstance(rule_id, str) validates dict.get('id') result from parsed YAML rule overrides. Guards against non-string IDs in base manifest.
Manifest merge: isinstance(rule_id, str) validates dict.get('id') result from overlay rule overrides. Filters malformed entries before merge.
Config parsing: isinstance(analysis_level, bool) guards against TOML booleans being accepted as ints (bool subclasses int in Python). System boundary validation.

exceptions.rationale: AST value dispatch: isinstance(node.args[0].value, str) checks the Python value inside an ast.Constant node. Post-parse structural dispatch, not a trust boundary.

AST value dispatch: isinstance(receiver, str) checks the return value from recursive _literalish_value call. Structural dispatch on the literal extraction result.

AST value dispatch: isinstance(call.args[0].value, str) checks the Python value inside an ast.Constant to extract keyword/arg names. Post-parse structural dispatch.

exceptions.rationale: Dict key checks on locally-built calls dict (via setdefault). Internal call-site index for order verification.

Coherence checker uses dict.get() with safe defaults when comparing tier assignments across manifest versions -- missing keys indicate absent tier config which is handled as a distinct case

exceptions.rationale: Taint resolver uses dict.get() to look up variable taint state with safe fallback -- unknown variables default to untainted which is the conservative correct behavior
Taint resolver uses dict.get() to look up variable taint state with safe fallback -- unknown variables default to untainted which is the conservative correct behavior
Scanner engine uses 'in' operator to check key presence in taint maps and module indexes before processing -- standard dict membership test for control flow in taint propagation
AST analysis helper uses match/case structural pattern matching on AST node types to classify control flow paths -- this is the idiomatic Python 3.10+ pattern for AST traversal
AST analysis uses match/case structural pattern matching on AST node types to classify statement types -- this is the idiomatic Python 3.10+ pattern for AST traversal
Metrics collector uses broad except Exception to handle arbitrary rule metric extraction failures gracefully -- emits zero metrics rather than crashing the regime fingerprint process
Coherence checker silently continues on individual tier comparison failures to report all downgrades rather than stopping at the first error -- graceful degradation for batch analysis
Coherence checker silently continues on individual tier comparison failures to report all upgrades rather than stopping at the first error -- graceful degradation for batch analysis
Manifest merge silently passes on individual overlay merge errors to process remaining overlays -- partial merge is preferable to total failure in monorepo scenarios
Metrics collector silently passes on individual metric extraction failures to collect remaining metrics -- partial metrics are preferable to no metrics for regime fingerprinting
Metrics collector silently passes on individual metric extraction failures to collect remaining metrics -- partial metrics are preferable to no metrics for regime fingerprinting
Taint resolver silently continues when individual function taint resolution fails -- processes remaining functions rather than aborting module-level taint assignment
Taint resolver silently passes when call target resolution fails -- unknown calls default to untainted which is the conservative correct behavior for taint analysis

exceptions.rationale: Introspection on arbitrary user objects: hasattr() is correct for checking tier stamp on objects whose type is not controlled by the framework
Introspection on arbitrary user objects: hasattr() is correct for checking tier stamp on objects whose type is not controlled by the framework

Internal TOML config loading: reading optional [wardline.governance] section with empty-dict default. Not a trust boundary — the config file is a trusted repository artefact, and the .get() pattern is the standard idiom for optional sections in config parsers.

Internal annotation-map coverage counter: 'in' used as dict membership check for coverage tallying, not as a trust gate. The keys are (file_path, qualname) tuples built from the scanner's own AST traversal — no user data, no structural-safety claim, no boundary.

SARIF emission null-coalescing fallback: 'self.degraded_commit_range or "unknown"' emits the literal string 'unknown' when git is unavailable, per §10.5 step 1 which requires degradedCommitRange to be present in SARIF output under non-normal law. Not a trust boundary — it's display fallback for a governance signal field where 'unknown' is the specified sentinel value.
